medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ year old female with severe RA who was referred by her ___ Rheumatologist to the ED for uncontrolled pain. She has trialed multiple medications without much improvement; noted emotional liability to Prednisone. Pt was re-started on Prednisone about 4 days prior at a dose of 10mg daily, reduced to 5mg due to emotional liability. Notable pain flare-ups reported, significant morning stiffness. Fatigue and waves of exhaustion noted.
medical_history.past_medical_history: RA, +CCP, -RF; attempted on multiple agents without much improvement and has notable emotional instability to Prednisone. Heavy menses s/p endometrial ablation. Previous diagnoses include thoracic outlet syndrome.
medical_history.family_history: Father with severe psoriasis, but no joint disease. Family history of diabetes and cancers.

physical_examination.general_examination: Initial vitals: 98.2, 139/87, 80, 18, 98% RA. Comfortable in bed, emotional. No acute distress. Alert, oriented.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Mild active synovitis mostly in b/l DIP and PIP and b/l knees with mild swelling. Motor and sensation intact. No significant neurological deficits.
